samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

arguments.description: Check for luminosity CLI and list directory contents

arguments.command: luminosity --help 2>&1 | head -50; echo "---CONFIG---"; luminosity config --help 2>&1 | head -50
arguments.description: Show luminosity CLI help and config subcommand help
Read the luminosity config value core.example
Inspect config.local.md contents with visible line endings
List contents of .luminosity directory

messages.content:
The `luminosity config get core.example` command fails with an error:

```
luminosity: config file '.luminosity/config.local.md' has malformed frontmatter: unterminated frontmatter block
```

The personal-level file `.luminosity/config.local.md` has an opening `---` but no closing `---`:
```
---
key: value
```

This breaks the whole `config get` resolution (personal-over-team), so it can't read anything. By contrast, the team-level file `.luminosity/config.md` is well-formed and sets `core.example: team-v`.

Want me to fix the malformed frontmatter in `config.local.md` (add the closing `---`) so `luminosity config get core.example` works, or would you rather handle that file yourself since it's your personal config?

sample.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

arguments.description: Check for luminosity CLI and list directory contents

arguments.command: luminosity --help 2>&1 | head -50; echo "---CONFIG---"; luminosity config --help 2>&1 | head -50
arguments.description: Show luminosity CLI help and config subcommand help
Read the luminosity config value core.example
Inspect config.local.md contents with visible line endings
List contents of .luminosity directory

attachments.dc6bfa8b62bac48d57d4211fad349a6f:
The `luminosity config get core.example` command fails with an error:

```
luminosity: config file '.luminosity/config.local.md' has malformed frontmatter: unterminated frontmatter block
```

The personal-level file `.luminosity/config.local.md` has an opening `---` but no closing `---`:
```
---
key: value
```

This breaks the whole `config get` resolution (personal-over-team), so it can't read anything. By contrast, the team-level file `.luminosity/config.md` is well-formed and sets `core.example: team-v`.

Want me to fix the malformed frontmatter in `config.local.md` (add the closing `---`) so `luminosity config get core.example` works, or would you rather handle that file yourself since it's your personal config?

samples.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

arguments.description: Check for luminosity CLI and inspect repo directory

arguments.command: luminosity config get core.example 2>&1; echo "---"; luminosity --help 2>&1 | head -50
arguments.description: Read luminosity config value core.example

messages.content: The `config.local.md` file has malformed frontmatter (unterminated block), so `luminosity config get` fails. Let me check the file directly.

arguments.description: Show raw contents of config.local.md with control characters visible

arguments.command: find <tmp>/luminosity-eval-przl669n/.luminosity -type f | while read f; do echo "== $f =="; cat "$f"; echo; done
arguments.description: List and print all files under .luminosity directory

messages.content:
The direct CLI command fails because `.luminosity/config.local.md` has malformed frontmatter (an unterminated `---` block), which blocks config resolution entirely.

Looking at the underlying files directly:
- `config.md` sets `core.example: team-v`
- `config.local.md` is broken — it opens a frontmatter block but never closes it, so `luminosity config get` errors out instead of returning a value

So **`core.example` = `team-v`** (from the base config), but the CLI itself can't currently report that due to the malformed local override file.

Want me to fix `config.local.md` (close the frontmatter block) so `luminosity config get core.example` works normally, or leave it as-is?

sample.input: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

messages.content: Read the luminosity config value core.example.

arguments.description: Read luminosity config value core.example
Show raw contents of config.local.md with control characters visible
List and print all files under .luminosity directory

attachments.fda132b0e3571f3f63c1c75da256c6b3:
The direct CLI command fails because `.luminosity/config.local.md` has malformed frontmatter (an unterminated `---` block), which blocks config resolution entirely.

Looking at the underlying files directly:
- `config.md` sets `core.example: team-v`
- `config.local.md` is broken — it opens a frontmatter block but never closes it, so `luminosity config get` errors out instead of returning a value

So **`core.example` = `team-v`** (from the base config), but the CLI itself can't currently report that due to the malformed local override file.

Want me to fix `config.local.md` (close the frontmatter block) so `luminosity config get core.example` works normally, or leave it as-is?

messages.content:
The team-level config (`.luminosity/config.md`) has `core.example: team-v`, but I can't confirm the resolved value because the personal-level config (`.luminosity/config.local.md`) has malformed frontmatter — it's missing the closing `---` delimiter:

```
---
key: value
```

Since `config get` resolves personal-over-team, it errors out instead of returning a value. This file is git-ignored (personal-level), so it's likely local scratch state rather than something to fix silently.

Do you want me to fix the frontmatter in `config.local.md` (add the closing `---`), or leave it as-is and just report the team-level value (`team-v`)?
